name: Greywatch
lore: The Last Free City — the one wall left, paved around a warm standing stone no one has ever dared move. 'Free' is polite: Greywatch pays — cattle south, grain east, its dead down the crypt stair. Two years ago the Charter nailed a bounty board to the old tithe-counting house and started hunting back.

npcs.name: Gorren the Smith
dialog: Steel for the wilds, friend. Out there even the rabbits belong to a king.
My grandfather forged tithe-chains. My father shod tithe-carts. I forge for the Charter. The family improves.
Royal work — Valdrenn work — is another thing entire. That steel remembers being a country.

npcs.name: Mara the Provisioner
dialog: Bread for the road, potions for when the road bites back.
Every party I provision goes up on that beam in chalk. The ones that come home, I cross out. Don't make me leave chalk standing.
The Council still drives cattle to the desert's edge every new moon. Call it 'the arrangement' if you like. I call it feeding the thing that starves us.
Pelts, tusks, gel — I pay board rate on all of it. Not the horn. If you're carrying Aelthir's horn, keep it wrapped and keep it moving.

npcs.name: Zella the Arcanist
dialog: A staff decides what you are: fire, frost, or mercy.
I have measured the portal-stone for thirty years. It has measured me right back. Neither of us will say what we found.
The arches were here before the walls. Before the tyrants, some say. Anyone who tells you they know what the portals are — sell them nothing on credit.
Casting takes focus — stand still, or the spell slips away.

npcs.name: Selvara the Enchanter
dialog: Every blade remembers what it was promised. I simply... remind it.
A finer piece holds more of my work — and holds it stronger.
I weave to the second degree here. For the third, seek the ember-witch past the desert.
Do you know what a king's ember does to a weaving? Neither do I. Bring me one and we'll both find out.
Tired of an old weaving? I can unpick it — curses too, for a price.

npcs.name: Jib the Carpenter
dialog: Block by block — that's how anything worth keeping gets built.
The Freehold's the first ground men have CLAIMED back since the Dividing. Build on it. That's not a hobby, that's a flag.
When the wood has no king and the fen runs dry, I'll build you a house with a door that locks from the inside only. Imagine that.
The Freehold arch is the one nearest my yard. Stack whatever you like out there — torches too, for the night work.

npcs.name: Warder Bren
dialog: Read the board before you walk an arch. The Charter posts every mark plain: the land, the beast, and the level you'd better be.
Three doors out of Greywatch, staggered like the board says: the Kingless Wood at level one, the Sunscour at four, the crypt stair at six. Walk them in that order.
The Kingless Wood first, greenhorn — marks at one through four, and the only crown out there is a poacher's red cap.
Past the wood's north gate lies the Strangler's March — old farmland the fen is drinking. March marks run five to seven. Past the far dyke it's fen rules — eight and up.
The Sunscour runs four to seven — the pride hunts the surface and the sand hides worse. Water is worth more than gold out there. Carry both.
Die past the wall and the stone brings you home. Your gear stays where you fell — the stone keeps no luggage. Run back quick.

npcs.name: Old Tam
dialog: I drove a tithe-cart forty years. Grain east, cattle south, our dead down the crypt stair. Nobody drives the dead-cart anymore. That's the Charter's doing, that is.
I saw the smoke when Valdrenn burned. Nine days of it. My cousin swore the ash came down warm here, ninety miles off.
'Every land has a king,' they taught us. Well. Turns out kings can be skinned. That's new.

npcs.name: Widow Kess
dialog: My husband's name is on the Tally Yard wall. The stone didn't give him back. They never say why it keeps some.
The Council smiles at your Charter in daylight and counts tribute owed by candlelight. Watch them.
The poachers in the wood sell to the kings' own creatures — skin the game AND inform on the hunters. A rope's too kind.

npcs.name: Pip
dialog: When I'm grown I'm taking the Red Mane's whole MANE. Warder Bren says start with slimes. Slimes! Ugh.
I touched the portal-stone once on a dare. It's warm! Like a wall a cat's been sleeping against.
A hunter sold a king's-guard trophy at Gorren's and the whole street came to look. It was still SMOKING.

npcs.name: Hunt-Master Corvyn
dialog: The Charter asks three things: take the mark, bring the proof, tell the truth about what you saw. The last one's hardest.
We are two years old. The tyrants are older than the roads. Plan accordingly — start at the board, left column, top.
No one has ever felled a king. Understand what it means when it happens — and it WILL — the tribute, the Council, the map, all of it changes. Be the change, hunter.
Sell your proof to any shop in the city — Gorren, Mara, whoever pays best. The Charter squares it with them after. The paperwork is my hunt. Go run yours.
Obedience failed. Defiance failed. We're the third thing.

npcs.name: Stonewarden Ivo
dialog: Mind the stone. Don't chip it, don't chalk it, don't pray at it too loud. It was here first and it'll be here last.
Folk leave coins on the stone for the dead it returns. The coins are always gone by morning. I don't take them. I don't ask.
Every arch in the world hums the same note, if you listen. Zella says that's nonsense. Zella hasn't listened.
